questions.question: In the feature branch workflow, what is the correct order of steps?
options: Create branch, push branch, make commits, open PR, merge
Create branch, make commits, push branch, open PR, merge
Push branch, create branch, make commits, merge, open PR
Make commits, create branch, push branch, merge, open PR
questions.answer: Create branch, make commits, push branch, open PR, merge

questions.question: Are pull requests a feature of Git or GitHub?
options: Git
GitHub
Both Git and GitHub
Neither; they are part of the operating system
questions.answer: GitHub

questions.question: What does the '-u' flag do in 'git push -u origin feature-login'?
options: It forces the push to overwrite remote changes
It sets the upstream tracking relationship between the local and remote branch
It creates the remote repository if it doesn't exist
It pushes all branches at once
questions.answer: It sets the upstream tracking relationship between the local and remote branch

questions.question: After running 'git push -u origin feature-login', what can you use for subsequent pushes on the same branch?
options: git push -u origin feature-login (every time)
git push origin main
git push
git send
questions.answer: git push

questions.question: Which command creates a new branch and switches to it?
options: git branch feature-login
git switch -c feature-login
git checkout feature-login
git push -u origin feature-login
questions.answer: git switch -c feature-login

questions.question: Why is it recommended NOT to push directly to main in real-world projects?
options: Git does not allow pushing to main
The main branch is read-only by default
The feature branch workflow allows code review before merging
Pushing to main automatically deletes all other branches
questions.answer: The feature branch workflow allows code review before merging

questions.question: When you push a feature branch to GitHub, what visual indicator appears on the repository page?
options: A red warning icon next to the branch name
A banner saying the branch had recent pushes with a button to create a pull request
An email notification is sent to all contributors
The branch is automatically merged into main
questions.answer: A banner saying the branch had recent pushes with a button to create a pull request

questions.question: When creating a pull request on GitHub, what should the 'base' branch be set to?
options: The feature branch you want to merge
main
origin
Any branch other than the compare branch
questions.answer: main

questions.question: When creating a pull request on GitHub, what should the 'compare' branch be set to?
options: main
The feature branch you want to merge
origin
The most recently updated branch
questions.answer: The feature branch you want to merge

questions.question: On a GitHub pull request page, which tab shows the diff of all changed files?
options: Conversation
Commits
Files changed
Checks
questions.answer: Files changed

questions.question: After merging a pull request on GitHub, what should you do with the remote feature branch?
options: Keep it for future reference
Rename it to 'merged-feature'
Delete it to clean up
Push more commits to it
questions.answer: Delete it to clean up

questions.question: After a PR is merged on GitHub, how do you update your local main branch?
options: git merge origin/main
git switch main && git pull
git fetch --all && git reset --hard
git switch main && git push
questions.answer: git switch main && git pull

questions.question: Which command safely deletes a local branch whose commits have already been merged into main?
options: git branch -D feature-login
git branch -d feature-login
git delete feature-login
git remove branch feature-login
questions.answer: git branch -d feature-login

questions.question: What is the difference between 'git branch -d' and 'git branch -D'?
options: There is no difference
-d deletes only if the branch is fully merged; -D force-deletes regardless
-D deletes only if the branch is fully merged; -d force-deletes regardless
-d deletes remote branches; -D deletes local branches
questions.answer: -d deletes only if the branch is fully merged; -D force-deletes regardless

questions.question: After deleting a remote branch on GitHub, what command removes the stale remote-tracking reference locally?
options: git branch -d origin/feature-login
git remote remove origin
git fetch --prune
git pull --delete
questions.answer: git fetch --prune

questions.question: Which command lists both local and remote-tracking branches?
options: git branch
git branch -a
git branch -r
git branch --list
questions.answer: git branch -a

questions.question: What does 'git branch -a' show after deleting a remote branch on GitHub but before running 'git fetch --prune'?
options: Only local branches
Nothing at all
The stale remote-tracking branch still appears in the listing
An error message about the missing branch
questions.answer: The stale remote-tracking branch still appears in the listing

questions.question: What feature do pull requests provide that a direct push to main does not?
options: Automatic deployment to production
Line-by-line code review and discussion before merging
Faster commit history
Automatic conflict resolution
questions.answer: Line-by-line code review and discussion before merging

questions.question: In the assignment, after merging the PR and pulling main locally, what files should appear on your local main branch?
options: Only login.html
login.html and login.css
Only login.css
No new files until you run git fetch
questions.answer: login.html and login.css

questions.question: Why is the local branch safe to delete with 'git branch -d' after the PR is merged?
options: Because the branch name is no longer valid
Because its commits are now part of main
Because GitHub automatically deletes local branches
Because the branch was never pushed to the remote
questions.answer: Because its commits are now part of main

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'Conversation' tab on a GitHub pull request?
options: To show the commit history of the entire repository
To display the overall PR description, comments, and activity
To run automated tests on the code
To compare branches across different repositories
questions.answer: To display the overall PR description, comments, and activity

questions.question: How can you open a pull request from the GitHub web interface?
options: Only through the 'Compare & pull request' banner
Only through the Pull Requests tab
Either through the 'Compare & pull request' banner or the Pull Requests tab
By running 'git pr create' in the terminal
questions.answer: Either through the 'Compare & pull request' banner or the Pull Requests tab

questions.question: In the full cycle practice, what is the correct final sequence after merging the PR on GitHub?
options: git pull, git switch main, git branch -d feature-footer, git fetch --prune
git switch main, git pull, git branch -d feature-footer, git fetch --prune
git fetch --prune, git switch main, git pull, git branch -d feature-footer
git branch -d feature-footer, git switch main, git pull, git fetch --prune
questions.answer: git switch main, git pull, git branch -d feature-footer, git fetch --prune

questions.question: What does 'git push -u origin feature-footer' do?
options: Pushes the feature-footer branch to the remote named 'origin' and sets upstream tracking
Pulls the feature-footer branch from the remote named 'origin'
Creates a new remote called 'feature-footer'
Deletes the feature-footer branch on the remote
questions.answer: Pushes the feature-footer branch to the remote named 'origin' and sets upstream tracking

questions.question: Which of the following best describes why 'git fetch --prune' is needed?
options: It downloads new commits from the remote
It removes local branches that have been merged
It removes local remote-tracking references for branches that no longer exist on the remote
It deletes the remote branch from GitHub
questions.answer: It removes local remote-tracking references for branches that no longer exist on the remote
